user_query:
I need a rigorous JSON validation and normalization pass over a small dataset. Please do all of the following:

1) Read and validate every JSON document found in the files under input/data/ as follows:
   - For .json files: treat the entire file as a single JSON document.
   - For .jsonl files: treat each non-empty line as an independent JSON document. Ignore blank or whitespace-only lines; do not treat them as documents.
   - For .jsonl lines, if a line looks like a comment (starts with '#'), skip it and do not treat it as a document.
   - For each document, attempt to parse it as JSON and capture whether it is valid or invalid. When invalid, capture the parsing error message as precisely as you can.

2) Produce a machine-checkable summary at output/report.json with this exact structure:
   {
     "documents_total": <int>,
     "documents_valid": <int>,
     "documents_invalid": <int>,
     "per_file": [
       {"path": "input/data/<filename>", "type": "json"|"jsonl", "documents": <int>, "valid": <int>, "invalid": <int>},
       ... one entry for every input file scanned ...
     ],
     "invalid_samples": [
       {"path": "input/data/<filename>", "line": <int|null>, "error_message": "...", "snippet": "..."},
       ... up to 5 examples taken from the invalid documents ...
     ]
   }
   - In per_file: for type "json", documents must equal 1, and valid+invalid must equal 1. For type "jsonl", documents must equal valid+invalid.
   - documents_total must equal the sum of per_file.documents; documents_valid and documents_invalid must match the sums of per_file.valid and per_file.invalid respectively.
   - For invalid_samples: include at most 5 examples; for .json files set line to 1; for .jsonl files set line to the 1-based line number of the failing document. Include a short snippet (<=80 chars) from the offending input (trim leading/trailing whitespace).

3) For every valid document, write a normalized copy under output/valid/:
   - For each valid .json file: write output/valid/<basename>.json containing a single pretty-printed JSON document (2-space indentation). Do not write any file if the .json file was invalid.
   - For each .jsonl file: write output/valid/<basename>.jsonl containing exactly one compact JSON object per valid line in the same order as the input (no extra blank lines). Only include the valid lines; do not include invalid lines or comments.

4) Write a short human-readable summary at output/README.md that includes:
   - A brief overview of what you validated and how (mention that .json was a single document and .jsonl was per line; blank/comment lines were skipped).
   - Counts for valid and invalid documents.
   - A bulleted list of common error patterns you observed and practical recommendations to fix them.

Please be careful to keep all file paths relative and to write all artifacts only under output/.
